content: Idk what Mister Softee did to get enough haters to necessitate a sign on an ice cream truck… but it’s also not my business.

media_attachments.description: Mister Softee ice cream truck with a giant decal / painting on the back that says “I heart my haters”

content: Wrote a note about an unglamorous encounter with a sheep https://kwon.nyc/notes/sheep/ and also curated some photos from the trip on which said encounter happened that are somewhat discordant with the bizarreness of the sheep encounter lol (no sheep pics) https://kwon.photos/banff-2026/
Unproven theory: the fewer seats a bánh mì shop has, the better the sandwich. If you walk in and there’s zero seating, get ready to (take away and) have the best bánh mì of your life.

One known exception is Ba Xuyên on 8th Av (Brooklyn Chinatown). I think they have 2 tables, but I’ve never been able to sit and eat there bc they are (rightfully) always occupied. But you can bring your bánh mì to Sunset Park which is one avenue away and eat it there.
Also, be very suspicious if they are NOT cash only!!
(Looking at my fellow immigrants and children of immigrants)
